items.title: Everything you need to know about Nasa's Artemis II mission
items.standfirst: The Artemis II mission is planned to last about 10 days and take its astronauts further into space than anyone has been before.
items.content: The Artemis II mission is planned to last about 10 days and take its astronauts further into space than anyone has been before.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts bound for moon after rocketing away on NASA's first lunar voyage in decades
items.standfirst: Four astronauts embarked on a high-stakes flight around the moon Wednesday, humanity’s first lunar voyage in more than half a century and the thrilling leadoff in NASA’s push toward a landing in two years. Journalist and aerospace expert Frédéric Castel reports from Cape Caranaval, Florida, USA.
items.content: Four astronauts embarked on a high-stakes flight around the moon Wednesday, humanity’s first lunar voyage in more than half a century and the thrilling leadoff in NASA’s push toward a landing in two years. Journalist and aerospace expert Frédéric Castel reports from Cape Caranaval, Florida, USA.

items.title: Artemis II launches historic flyby lunar mission
items.standfirst: 🚀🌛 Four astronauts – 3 Americans and 1 Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years.
items.content: 🚀🌛 Four astronauts – 3 Americans and 1 Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years.

items.title: In a thunderous launch, Artemis II astronauts leave Earth. Here's what's next
items.standfirst: NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. Here's what to expect over their roughly 10-day journey.
items.content: NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. Here's what to expect over their roughly 10-day journey. (Image credit: Bill Ingalls)

items.title: What happened in Artemis II’s launch? #science
items.standfirst: Artemis II sends astronauts around the Moon NASA’s Artemis II mission launched four astronauts on a crewed lunar flyby, blasting off on a massive rocket from the Kennedy Space Center. The flight is designed to take the crew around the Moon—marking the first crewed journey beyond Earth orbit in…

The flight is designed to take the crew around the Moon—marking the first crewed journey beyond Earth orbit in…

items.title: Artemis II Mission Launches Successfully
items.standfirst: The crew of Artemis II will not descend to the moon, but their capsule will fly over the far side of its surface.
items.content: The crew of Artemis II will not descend to the moon, but their capsule will fly over the far side of its surface.

items.standfirst: Artemis II lifted off on a crewed mission around the Moon for the first time in more than half a century, marking a major step in NASA’s plan to expand human spaceflight. The flight sent four astronauts aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft on a roughly 10 day journey that will take them on an…

items.title: Why the Artemis II crew played cards right before launch
items.standfirst: Minutes before departing for the launch pad on Wednesday, the Artemis II crew completed one final task: a game of cards. Retired NASA astronaut and U.S. Navy SEAL Chris Cassidy joins CBS News to discuss the mission.
items.content: Minutes before departing for the launch pad on Wednesday, the Artemis II crew completed one final task: a game of cards. Retired NASA astronaut and U.S. Navy SEAL Chris Cassidy joins CBS News to discuss the mission.

items.title: Watch: Artemis II launches on historic moon mission | Special Report
items.standfirst: The Artemis II mission lifted off on Wednesday from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report on the launch.
items.content: The Artemis II mission lifted off on Wednesday from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report on the launch.

items.title: Here's who's on the Artemis II crew headed toward the moon
items.standfirst: The first woman, Black man and Canadian to fly around the moon are on Artemis II, which launched Wednesday evening. Former NASA astronaut Ron Garan has more about the mission.
items.content: The first woman, Black man and Canadian to fly around the moon are on Artemis II, which launched Wednesday evening. Former NASA astronaut Ron Garan has more about the mission.

items.title: What's the status of the Artemis II rocket?
items.standfirst: Four astronauts are on a historic, unprecedented mission around the far side of the moon and back after the Artemis II launched on Wednesday. Derrick Pitts, chief astronomer at the Franklin Institute, joins CBS News to discuss and CBS News' Cristian Benavides and Kris Van Cleave report.
items.content: Four astronauts are on a historic, unprecedented mission around the far side of the moon and back after the Artemis II launched on Wednesday. Derrick Pitts, chief astronomer at the Franklin Institute, joins CBS News to discuss and CBS News' Cristian Benavides and Kris Van Cleave report.

items.title: Huge crowds gather to watch Artemis II launch
items.standfirst: CBS News' Rob Marciano reports from Space View Park, where a huge crowd gathered to watch the Artemis II launch.
items.content: CBS News' Rob Marciano reports from Space View Park, where a huge crowd gathered to watch the Artemis II launch.

items.title: NASA's Artemis II Lifts Off in Landmark Moon Mission
items.standfirst: NASA astronauts lifted off on a 10-day journey that will slingshot them around the moon, marking humanity’s return to the lunar vicinity for the first time in more than half a century. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: NASA astronauts lifted off on a 10-day journey that will slingshot them around the moon, marking humanity’s return to the lunar vicinity for the first time in more than half a century. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Artemis II launches, sending NASA astronauts on historic mission around the moon
items.standfirst: Four astronauts lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, kicking off NASA's Artemis II mission to the moon. Tony Dokoupil, Mark Strassmann, Bill Harwood and Rob Marciano have more.
items.content: Four astronauts lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, kicking off NASA's Artemis II mission to the moon. Tony Dokoupil, Mark Strassmann, Bill Harwood and Rob Marciano have more.

items.title: Kids on Artemis II launch: "It was the coolest thing I've seen ever"
items.standfirst: Crowds of people watched the Artemis II rocket take off on Wednesday evening from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida. CBS News' Rob Marciano spoke with one family for their reaction.
items.content: Crowds of people watched the Artemis II rocket take off on Wednesday evening from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida. CBS News' Rob Marciano spoke with one family for their reaction.

items.title: Watch: NASA's Artemis II launches for historic mission
items.standfirst: In a historic mission with four crew members onboard, NASA launched its Artemis II rocket at 6:35 p.m. on Wednesday. Watch the liftoff.
items.content: In a historic mission with four crew members onboard, NASA launched its Artemis II rocket at 6:35 p.m. on Wednesday. Watch the liftoff.

items.title: Artemis II launches historic flyby lunar mission with diverse crew
items.standfirst: Four astronauts – three Americans and one Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years. The 32-storey rocket soared from Kennedy Space Center, marking the start of a decade-long plan for sustainable Moon exploration and a future landing near the south pole.
items.content: Four astronauts – three Americans and one Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years. The 32-storey rocket soared from Kennedy Space Center, marking the start of a decade-long plan for sustainable Moon exploration and a future landing near the south pole.

items.title: Watch: Retired astronaut chokes up as Artemis II passes final launch status check
items.standfirst: NASA ran its final "go/no-go poll," ahead of the planned Artemis II flight on Wednesday. Retired NASA astronaut Peggy Whitson and CBS News senior space consultant Bill Harwood reacted to the command.
items.content: NASA ran its final "go/no-go poll," ahead of the planned Artemis II flight on Wednesday. Retired NASA astronaut Peggy Whitson and CBS News senior space consultant Bill Harwood reacted to the command.

items.title: Kid with NASA aspirations prepares for Artemis II launch: "I'm so obsessed with space"
items.standfirst: People from across the country have traveled to the Kennedy Space Center area ahead of the planned Artemis II launch, including young people and kids. CBS News' Rob Marciano talks with an Atlanta dad and son from Space View Park in Florida.
items.content: People from across the country have traveled to the Kennedy Space Center area ahead of the planned Artemis II launch, including young people and kids. CBS News' Rob Marciano talks with an Atlanta dad and son from Space View Park in Florida.

items.title: Inside the history of the Artemis II launch
items.standfirst: NASA's Artemis II crew is set for a historic liftoff on Wednesday. Jack Kiraly, director of government relations for the Planetary Society, and Harrison Schmitt, an Apollo 17 astronaut, join CBS News to preview the Artemis II launch.
items.content: NASA's Artemis II crew is set for a historic liftoff on Wednesday. Jack Kiraly, director of government relations for the Planetary Society, and Harrison Schmitt, an Apollo 17 astronaut, join CBS News to preview the Artemis II launch.

items.title: Artemis II launch is "America at its finest," Florida Rep. Mike Haridopolos says
items.standfirst: Florida Rep. Mike Haridopolos's district includes the Kennedy Space Center, where Artemis II is launching from on Wednesday. He joins CBS News to discuss the mission ahead of liftoff.
items.content: Florida Rep. Mike Haridopolos's district includes the Kennedy Space Center, where Artemis II is launching from on Wednesday. He joins CBS News to discuss the mission ahead of liftoff.

items.title: How to watch the Artemis II launch
items.standfirst: As launch time approaches for NASA's first moonshot in more than half a century, anticipation is building for the Artemis II mission. Here's how to watch today's liftoff.

items.title: NASA Astronauts Reach Safe Orbit in Historic Moon Mission
items.standfirst: NASA’s crew of astronauts launched to space and reached a stable orbit, kicking off a landmark journey that will take them closer to the lunar surface than anyone has been in more than 50 years.
items.content: NASA’s crew of astronauts launched to space and reached a stable orbit, kicking off a landmark journey that will take them closer to the lunar surface than anyone has been in more than 50 years.

items.title: These Are the 4 Artemis II Astronauts Leading the Historic Return to the Moon

items.title: ‘Back to the Stone Ages’: #Trump says #Iran war nearly over in national #address
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump delivered his first nationwide address since the start of the war in the Middle East on Wednesday. Trump claimed the US was “very close” to achieving its objectives in Iran and vowed to hit the country “extremely hard” in the coming days.
items.content: US President Donald Trump delivered his first nationwide address since the start of the war in the Middle East on Wednesday. Trump claimed the US was “very close” to achieving its objectives in Iran and vowed to hit the country “extremely hard” in the coming days.

items.title: Trump says Iran war will wrap up "very shortly," but warns of more strikes in coming weeks
items.standfirst: President Trump said in a speech that the U.S. will complete its military mission in Iran "very shortly," and that U.S. forces have achieved "overwhelming victories."
items.content: President Trump said in a speech that the U.S. will complete its military mission in Iran "very shortly," and that U.S. forces have achieved "overwhelming victories."

items.title: Oil jumps and shares fall after US president address
items.standfirst: The US president says he will bring Iran "back to the Stone Age" but failed to say how the war will end.
items.content: The US president says he will bring Iran "back to the Stone Age" but failed to say how the war will end.

items.title: Iran vows 'more destructive' attacks after Trump threats
items.standfirst: Iran on Thursday threatened "crushing" attacks on the US and Israel, firing missiles at Tel Aviv after US President Donald Trump vowed to bomb the Islamic republic "back to the Stone Ages". The war, which erupted more than a month ago with US-Israeli strikes on Iran, has spread throughout the Middle East and roiled the global economy, impacting hundreds of millions worldwide. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran, Iran.
items.content: Iran on Thursday threatened "crushing" attacks on the US and Israel, firing missiles at Tel Aviv after US President Donald Trump vowed to bomb the Islamic republic "back to the Stone Ages". The war, which erupted more than a month ago with US-Israeli strikes on Iran, has spread throughout the Middle East and roiled the global economy, impacting hundreds of millions worldwide. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran, Iran.

Trump's remarks in a televised address Wednesday dimmed hopes of a swift end to the war in the Middle East. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Trump hints at timeline for war with Iran during address to nation

items.content: During his address to the nation, President Donald Trump threatened to send Iran "back to the Stone Ages" and spoke about possibility for negotiations. NBC News’ Monica Alba recaps the highlights from Trump’s address.

items.title: Trump says U.S. strikes will send Iran back to 'Stone Ages'
items.standfirst: President Trump says the U.S. will strike Iran "extremely hard" in the coming days, bringing them back to the "Stone Ages." Trump also says the killing of top leadership in the country has led to a regime change.
items.content: President Trump says the U.S. will strike Iran "extremely hard" in the coming days, bringing them back to the "Stone Ages." Trump also says the killing of top leadership in the country has led to a regime change.

items.title: Trump pledges to 'finish the job' in Iran in national address but offers no exit plan
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said US forces would keep hitting Iran "very hard" in the next two or three weeks and bring the country "back to the Stone Ages" in a televised address on Wednesday night, even as he argued that Washington had accomplished its goals but offered no clear timeline for ending the monthlong war.
items.content: President Donald Trump said US forces would keep hitting Iran "very hard" in the next two or three weeks and bring the country "back to the Stone Ages" in a televised address on Wednesday night, even as he argued that Washington had accomplished its goals but offered no clear timeline for ending the monthlong war.

items.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president Delcy Rodríguez
items.standfirst: US moves towards reestablishing working relations between two countries after abducting President Nicolás MaduroThe US has lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president, Delcy Rodríguez, in the latest step towards normalising relations between the two countries after US forces abducted her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro, and his wife.The couple were taken to New York after their abduction in January to face charges of alleged drug trafficking, to which both have pleaded not guilty. Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
items.standfirst: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.
items.content: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.(Image credit: Ariana Cubillos)

items.title: Trump administration lifts sanctions on Venezuela’s interim leader
items.standfirst: The US has lifted sanctions on Delcy Rodriguez, Venezuela’s interim president, in a significant shift following the ouster of Nicolas Maduro. The move comes as relations between Washington and Caracas begin to thaw, with Rodriguez taking steps to open the country’s energy sector to US companies after assuming power earlier this year.
items.content: The US has lifted sanctions on Delcy Rodriguez, Venezuela’s interim president, in a significant shift following the ouster of Nicolas Maduro. The move comes as relations between Washington and Caracas begin to thaw, with Rodriguez taking steps to open the country’s energy sector to US companies after assuming power earlier this year.

Plus, how rap lyrics were used to help sentence a man to deathDon’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up hereGood morning.Donald Trump declared the month-long US-Israeli war against Iran a success “nearing completion” in his prime time address to the nation on Wednesday evening – despite the conflict wreaking global economic chaos, damaging transatlantic alliances and hitting his approval ratings.What is the significance of Trump’s comments on HEU? The apparent decision to leave it appears to conflict with his assertions that a key war aim was to ensure Iran could never make a nuclear bomb.Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.What has Houston said about the rocket’s deployment? Flight controllers confirmed that all four solar arrays, which will provide the spacecraft with continuous electrical power throughout its lunar journey, were deployed successfully. Continue reading...

items.title: Thursday briefing: ​Why does Donald Trump have it in for the UK?
items.standfirst: In today’s newsletter: Britain’s refusal to be drawn into ​t​he Iran war has triggered a backlash from the White House​, with the president’s fury expos​ing the fragility of this once unshakeable partnershipGood morning. Another week, another tirade against the UK from Donald Trump. The US-Israeli conflict with Iran has further inflamed tensions in the special relationship, which was already under strain from attacks by the capricious US leader. So far this week, Trump has once again mocked the UK’s navy, instructed allies worried about jet fuel supplies to take it from the strait of Hormuz themselves, and announced that the US is considering leaving Nato.The outbursts have become a pattern since the war with Iran began – and mark a departure from the unlikely friendly relationship Starmer and Trump have enjoyed until now.Middle East | Donald Trump used a prime-time address to the nation to declare the month-long war in Iran a success “nearing completion”, despite a spiralling conflict that has caused economic turmoil across the globe, fractured transatlantic alliances and eroded the president’s approval ratings.UK politics | The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels.Nasa | Nasa’s moon rocket Artemis II launched on Wednesday evening, carrying astronauts to the moon for the first time in almost 54 years.BBC | The BBC confirmed in a statement it was first made aware of a police investigation into historical allegations of sexual abuse by Scott Mills in 2017.NHS | Claims by Palantir that concerns over the US data analytics company’s multimillion-pound NHS contract are “ideologically motivated” have been rejected by the chair of a parliamentary committee. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump claims Iran war ‘nearing completion’ and seeks to justify conflict in prime time address
items.standfirst: Markets sink after president offers little detail on how he intends to wind down conflict over next two to three weeksMiddle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump used a prime time address to the nation on Wednesday evening to declare the month-long war in Iran a success “nearing completion”, despite a spiraling conflict that has caused economic turmoil across the globe, fractured transatlantic alliances and eroded the president’s approval ratings.In remarks from the White House, Trump argued that the US’s “little journey” to Iran had nearly accomplished “all of America’s military objectives”, but offered little clarity on how he planned to wind down the conflict over the next “two to three weeks”. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel hits Iran with waves of attacks and says it killed top Hezbollah commander
items.standfirst: War shows little sign of easing despite Donald Trump claiming Iranian leadership ‘just asked’ for ceasefireMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael unleashed two waves of attacks on Tehran and said it had killed a senior Hezbollah commander on Wednesday with little sign of the war easing up despite Donald Trump repeating a claim that Iran’s leadership was seeking a ceasefire.The US president, writing on social media, said that Iran’s president had “just asked” for a ceasefire and that American troops would be “out of Iran pretty quickly” as he sought to extricate the US from the war. He indicated that he was not concerned about leaving Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium (HEU) – often cited as a justification for the war - in its presumed underground hiding place, arguing it could be monitored by satellite. Continue reading...
items.content: War shows little sign of easing despite Donald Trump claiming Iranian leadership ‘just asked’ for ceasefireMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael unleashed two waves of attacks on Tehran and said it had killed a senior Hezbollah commander on Wednesday with little sign of the war easing up despite Donald Trump repeating a claim that Iran’s leadership was seeking a ceasefire.The US president, writing on social media, said that Iran’s president had “just asked” for a ceasefire and that American troops would be “out of Iran pretty quickly” as he sought to extricate the US from the war. He indicated that he was not concerned about leaving Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium (HEU) – often cited as a justification for the war - in its presumed underground hiding place, arguing it could be monitored by satellite. Continue reading...

items.title: Oil price falls and markets rally after Trump says Iran war over in ‘two to three weeks’
items.standfirst: US president’s claim that conflict is nearing end prompts 15% drop in Brent crude and stock market climb in AsiaBusiness live – latest updatesOil prices tumbled and stock markets have rallied across the world after Donald Trump said the war in Iran would end in “two to three weeks”.Brent crude, the international benchmark for oil, fell as low as $98.35 a barrel on Wednesday, down more than 15% on the previous day and its lowest level in a week. It later recovered some ground, ending the day at $102. Continue reading...
